lesson_text:
STEP 1
TITLE: The Core Problem
ANCHOR: kth positive integer
CONTEXT: Return the kth positive integer that is missing
ATTENTION: underline
EMPHASIS: medium
EXPLANATION: We need to find the k-th missing number. If the array had no missing numbers, the value at any index 'i' would simply be 'i + 1'.

STEP 2
TITLE: The Missing Count Insight
ANCHOR: [2,3,4,7,11]
CONTEXT: Input: arr = [2,3,4,7,11]
ATTENTION: rectangle
EMPHASIS: high
EXPLANATION: Look at the number 7 at index 3. In a perfect array, index 3 should hold the number 4. Since it holds 7, we know exactly (7 - 4) = 3 numbers are missing before it!

STEP 3
TITLE: Deciding Where to Search
ANCHOR: k = 5
CONTEXT: k = 5
ATTENTION: circle
EMPHASIS: high
EXPLANATION: Since we need the 5th missing number, and only 3 numbers are missing before the 7, we immediately know our target must be in the right half of the array.

STEP 4
TITLE: The Optimal Strategy
ANCHOR: strictly increasing order
CONTEXT: sorted in a strictly increasing order
ATTENTION: rectangle
EMPHASIS: high
EXPLANATION: Because the array is sorted, we don't have to count one by one. We can use Binary Search to find the exact boundary in O(log N) time!
